Defines structures and functions for creating and managing a health graph card in the Pebble firmware.

# Purpose
The code defines a C header file that provides the structure and functionality for managing a `HealthGraphCard`. This card is a user interface component that displays health-related data over a week. The file includes type definitions, function prototypes, and data structures necessary for creating, configuring, and managing the lifecycle of a `HealthGraphCard`. It imports necessary dependencies from other modules such as `health_data.h`, `fonts.h`, `layer.h`, and `time.h` to support its operations.

Key components include the `HealthGraphCard` structure, which holds data such as weekly statistics, timestamps, and graphical attributes like fonts and colors. The `HealthGraphCardConfig` structure is used to configure the card with titles, average information, and update functions. The file also defines an enumeration `HealthGraphIndex` to manage the indexing of days and averages. Functions provided include [`health_graph_card_create`](<#health_graph_card_create>), [`health_graph_card_destroy`](<#health_graph_card_destroy>), [`health_graph_card_configure`](<#health_graph_card_configure>), [`health_graph_card_cycle_selected`](<#health_graph_card_cycle_selected>), and [`health_graph_format_weekday_prefix`](<#health_graph_format_weekday_prefix>), which handle the creation, destruction, configuration, selection cycling, and formatting of the card, respectively. This header file is intended to be included in other C source files to facilitate the use of the `HealthGraphCard` component.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `health_data.h`
- `applib/fonts/fonts.h`
- `applib/ui/layer.h`
- `util/time/time.h`


# Data Structures

---
### HealthGraphIndex
- **Type**: ``enum``
- **Members**:
    - ``HealthGraphIndex_Sunday``: Represents the index for Sunday.
    - ``HealthGraphIndex_Monday``: Represents the index for Monday.
    - ``HealthGraphIndex_Saturday``: Represents the index for Saturday.
    - ``HealthGraphIndex_Average``: Represents the index for the average, calculated as Sunday index plus the number of days per week.
    - ``HealthGraphIndexCount``: Represents the total count of indices in the `HealthGraphIndex` enumeration.
- **Description**: Defines indices for days of the week and an average index for use in health graph data structures, with specific values for Sunday, Monday, Saturday, and an average index calculated from Sunday.


---
### HealthGraphCard
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``layer``: Contains the graphical layer information for the card.
    - ``stats``: Holds weekly statistics data.
    - ``day_data``: Stores daily data for each day of the week plus one extra day.
    - ``data_timestamp``: Records the time in UTC seconds when the data is applicable.
    - ``data_max``: Represents the maximum data value for the week.
    - ``title_font``: Specifies the font used for the title text.
    - ``legend_font``: Specifies the font used for the legend text.
    - ``day_chars``: Points to a string of characters representing the days of the week.
    - ``title``: Points to the title text of the card.
    - ``info_avg``: Points to the average information text.
    - ``inactive_color``: Defines the color used for inactive elements.
    - ``info_update``: Function pointer for updating information on the card.
    - ``info_buffer_size``: Specifies the size of the buffer for information updates.
    - ``current_day``: Indicates the current day of the week, with Sunday as 0.
    - ``selection``: Represents the current selection index for the graph.
- **Description**: Represents a graphical card that displays health-related data for a week, including daily statistics, fonts for text display, and configuration for updating and displaying information. It includes a graphical layer, weekly statistics, daily data, timestamp, maximum data value, fonts for title and legend, day characters, title, average information, inactive color, update function, buffer size, current day, and selection index.


---
### HealthGraphCardData
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``stats``: Contains weekly statistics data.
    - ``timestamp``: Stores the time at which the data applies in UTC seconds.
    - ``day_data``: Points to an array of daily data values.
    - ``default_max``: Holds the default maximum value for the data.
- **Description**: Represents the data structure for storing health graph card information, including weekly statistics, a timestamp for the data, a pointer to daily data values, and a default maximum value for the data.


---
### HealthGraphCardConfig
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``title``: A pointer to a constant character string for the card's title.
    - ``info_avg``: A pointer to a constant character string for average information.
    - ``graph_data``: A pointer to a `HealthGraphCardData` structure containing graph data.
    - ``info_update``: A function pointer for updating card information.
    - ``info_buffer_size``: The size of the buffer for information updates.
    - ``inactive_color``: A constant `GColor` for the inactive state color.
- **Description**: Defines the configuration for a health graph card, including title, average information, graph data, update function, buffer size, and inactive color.


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### health\_graph\_card\_create<!-- {{#callable_declaration:health_graph_card_create}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/health/health_graph_card.h#L78>)

Creates a new health graph card instance.
- **Description**: Use this function to create a new instance of a health graph card, which is used to display health data graphically. This function allocates memory for the card and initializes it with the provided configuration. Ensure that the `config` parameter is correctly set up before calling this function. The caller is responsible for managing the memory of the created card, including its eventual destruction using the appropriate function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `config`: A pointer to a `HealthGraphCardConfig` structure that contains the configuration settings for the health graph card. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership of the configuration data.
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to a newly created `HealthGraphCard` instance, or null if memory allocation fails.
- **See Also**: [`health_graph_card_create`](<health_graph_card.c.md#health_graph_card_create>)  (Implementation)

### health\_graph\_card\_destroy<!-- {{#callable_declaration:health_graph_card_destroy}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/health/health_graph_card.h#L81>)

Releases resources associated with a HealthGraphCard.
- **Description**: Use this function to release all resources associated with a `HealthGraphCard` when it is no longer needed. This function must be called to prevent memory leaks. It is safe to call this function with a null pointer, in which case it will do nothing.
- **Inputs**:
    - `graph_card`: A pointer to a `HealthGraphCard` object to be destroyed. Must not be used after this function is called. If null, the function does nothing.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`health_graph_card_destroy`](<health_graph_card.c.md#health_graph_card_destroy>)  (Implementation)


---
### health\_graph\_card\_configure<!-- {{#callable_declaration:health_graph_card_configure}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/health/health_graph_card.h#L84>)

Configures a health graph card with specified settings.
- **Description**: Use this function to set or update the configuration of a `HealthGraphCard` object. It applies the settings from the provided `HealthGraphCardConfig` to the `HealthGraphCard`. This function must be called with valid pointers for both parameters. If either parameter is null, the function returns immediately without making changes. The function updates various fields of the `HealthGraphCard` based on the non-null fields of the `HealthGraphCardConfig`. It is important to ensure that the `HealthGraphCard` is properly initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `graph_card`: A pointer to a `HealthGraphCard` object to configure. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership.
    - `config`: A pointer to a `HealthGraphCardConfig` structure containing the configuration settings. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`health_graph_card_configure`](<health_graph_card.c.md#health_graph_card_configure>)  (Implementation)


---
### health\_graph\_card\_cycle\_selected<!-- {{#callable_declaration:health_graph_card_cycle_selected}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/health/health_graph_card.h#L87>)

Cycles the selection index of a health graph card.
- **Description**: Use this function to change the current selection index of a `HealthGraphCard` to the next logical index. It cycles through the days of the week and includes an average index. If the current selection is Sunday, it changes to the average index. If the current selection is the average index, it changes to Monday. For other days, it progresses to the next day of the week. This function must be called with a valid `HealthGraphCard` pointer.
- **Inputs**:
    - `graph_card`: A pointer to a `HealthGraphCard` structure. Must not be null. The function modifies the `selection` field of this structure.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`health_graph_card_cycle_selected`](<health_graph_card.c.md#health_graph_card_cycle_selected>)  (Implementation)


---
### health\_graph\_format\_weekday\_prefix<!-- {{#callable_declaration:health_graph_format_weekday_prefix}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/health/health_graph_card.h#L90>)

Formats a string with a prefix of the current weekday selection.
- **Description**: Use this function to format a string that includes a prefix representing the current weekday selection from a `HealthGraphCard`. This function is useful when you need to display or log the selected weekday in a human-readable format. Ensure that the `HealthGraphCard` is properly initialized and configured before calling this function. The function writes the formatted string into the provided buffer, taking care not to exceed the specified buffer size.
- **Inputs**:
    - `graph_card`: A pointer to a `HealthGraphCard` structure. Must not be null. The function uses this to determine the current weekday selection.
    - `buffer`: A pointer to a character array where the formatted string will be written. Must not be null. The caller must ensure the buffer is large enough to hold the formatted string.
    - `buffer_size`: The size of the buffer in bytes. Must be greater than zero. The function will not write more than this number of bytes to the buffer.
- **Output**: Returns the number of characters written to the buffer, excluding the null terminator.
- **See Also**: [`health_graph_format_weekday_prefix`](<health_graph_card.c.md#health_graph_format_weekday_prefix>)  (Implementation)
